report_name: Widespread "Shai-Hulud" Supply Chain Attack on TanStack Compromises OpenAI

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A criminal group that orchestrated the Shai-Hulud supply chain attack, compromising the TanStack npm library and other open-source packages to inject a self-propagating worm.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: TeamPCP compromised the TanStack npm package and published a new, malicious version to the public npm registry, which was then downloaded by downstream users like OpenAI.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: The Shai-Hulud worm, once executed on employee devices, searched for and exfiltrated credentials, API keys, and other secrets from locations like .env files, shell history, and Git configurations.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: The Shai-Hulud worm was executed as part of a `post-install` script within the malicious npm package, leveraging the JavaScript (Node.js) interpreter.

malware.name: Shai-Hulud
malware.description: A self-propagating worm designed for credential harvesting, supply chain poisoning, and data exfiltration. It was injected into the TanStack npm library and other open-source packages.

campaigns.name: Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Campaign
campaigns.description: A widespread software supply chain campaign orchestrated by TeamPCP using the Shai-Hulud worm to compromise npm packages, including TanStack, and infect downstream users like OpenAI.

identities.name: OpenAI
identities.description: A high-profile victim of the Shai-Hulud supply chain attack. Two employee devices were compromised after downloading malicious npm packages, leading to the theft of credentials and secrets.

identities.name: TanStack
identities.description: A popular JavaScript library ecosystem that was a primary target of the Shai-Hulud supply chain attack. A malicious version of the library was published to the npm registry.

infrastructure.name: Malicious npm Package Repository
infrastructure.description: The public npm registry was used as a distribution vector to host and serve malicious versions of the TanStack library and other packages containing the Shai-Hulud worm.

indicators.name: Shai-Hulud Credential File Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Shai-Hulud Credential File Access
id: 2b8c1f9a-7d3e-4b1c-8a9f-0e2d5c6b7a8d
status: experimental
description: Detects access to common developer credential files, such as .git-credentials and .npmrc, which were targeted by the Shai-Hulud worm.
references:
  - https://thecyberwire.com/newsletters/daily-briefing/15/94
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-17
tags:
  - attack.t1552
  - attack.credential_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate access by developer tools like git or npm.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '/.git-credentials'
      - '/.npmrc'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/git'
      - '/npm'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects access to developer credential files like .git-credentials and .npmrc, a behavior of the Shai-Hulud worm.
